title: 250 Years of American State Capitalism
gist: Marking 250 years since the Declaration of Independence, Shang-Jin Wei argues the popular narrative of American laissez-faire capitalism is a myth: from its founding the US has run a hybrid model in which the state directs, subsidizes, and occasionally bails out private enterprise to serve national priorities.

insights.claim: The idea that US prosperity was built on laissez-faire capitalism is one of the great myths of American economic life; from its founding the US has run a hybrid model in which the state directs, subsidizes, and occasionally bails out private enterprise in service of national priorities.
insights.speaker: Shang-Jin Wei
insights.so_what: Reframes US economic history as state-directed rather than free-market, a lens relevant to assessing the legitimacy and durability of current industrial-policy and subsidy programs (e.g., CHIPS-style interventions) as continuity rather than departure.
insights.quote: one of the great myths of American economic life: that the country’s prosperity was built on laissez-faire capitalism
insights.locator: NEW YORK—As the United States marks 250 years

insights.claim: Wei ties the reassessment of America's state-capitalist history to the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence in 2026, framing it as a timely occasion to revisit the free-market narrative.
insights.speaker: Shang-Jin Wei
insights.so_what: Signals a likely wave of historical/economic-policy commentary tied to the US semiquincentennial that may shape 2026 political and policy narratives around industrial policy.
insights.quote: As the United States marks 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence this summer, there is no better time to reckon
insights.locator: NEW YORK—As the United States marks 250 years
